title: Snap finally debuts its long-awaited AR glasses, Specs, and, oof, they aren’t cheap
slides: Snap finally showed Specs—but who will actually buy them?
The glasses overlay digital objects onto the real world.
Their $1,399 price tag matches a high-end smartphone.
You now need to justify AR’s value for your own wallet.
Try a demo before deciding if the price makes sense.
thread_text:
1. Who needs AR glasses?

Snap’s new Specs cost $1,399—same as a premium phone. That price forces a real choice.

2. Digital objects overlaid

The glasses blend digital items with your real view. No phone in hand required.

3. Justifying the price tag

At $1,399, you compare it to a high-end smartphone. The question is what AR adds that your phone doesn’t do.

4. Your wallet decides

You now must weigh AR’s value for your own spending. The demo might change your math.

5. Try the demo first

Visit a store to test the Specs this week. Does the experience justify the cost for you?
video_script: Snap’s AR glasses cost more than a phone.
They overlay digital objects onto the real world.
The price tag demands a personal justification.
Try a demo before you decide to buy.
Does AR’s value match your wallet’s needs?

script: Snap’s AR glasses cost more than a phone.
They overlay digital objects onto the real world.
The price tag demands a personal justification.
Try a demo before you decide to buy.
Does AR’s value match your wallet’s needs?

instagram.caption:
Snap’s new Specs cost $1,399—the same as a premium phone 💸

They overlay digital objects onto your real view, no phone needed.

But at that price, you have to ask what AR adds that your phone doesn’t already do.

For creators: filming hands-free might change your shot setup, but now you must justify a high-end gadget against your camera gear.

Does the demo convince you to swap your smartphone budget for AR glasses?

carousel_slides.hook: Would you pay $1,399 for AR glasses?
carousel_slides.description: Snap's Specs cost as much as a premium phone, forcing you to justify AR's value.
The glasses overlay digital items onto your real view, no phone needed.
At a high-end price, compare what AR adds that your phone doesn't already do.
You must weigh AR's worth for your own spending—a demo could change the math.
Visit a store to try Specs this week and see if the experience justifies the cost.
